# Macronutrients and energy

> *Starter chapter — expand or replace. It exists here to demonstrate how chapters
> join the table of contents.*

## Energy density

The macronutrients differ in the energy they yield per gram:

| Macronutrient | Energy (kcal/g) |
|---|---|
| Carbohydrate | ~4 |
| Protein | ~4 |
| Fat | ~9 |
| Alcohol | ~7 |

Fat is the most energy-dense, which is why fat-rich foods raise total intake quickly.

## Carbohydrate

Carbohydrate is the body's primary fuel. Quality matters as much as quantity — wholegrains
and legumes behave very differently from refined sugars.

## Protein

Protein supports tissue maintenance and has the highest thermic effect of the macronutrients,
contributing modestly to energy expenditure.

## Fat

Dietary fat is essential but energy-dense; the *type* of fat (unsaturated vs saturated) is a
key target in cardiometabolic counselling.

::: {.callout-note}
## Linking back
These ideas feed directly into the [energy balance](energy-balance.qmd) chapter, where we turn
intake and expenditure into a concrete plan.
:::
